name: Somalia
avoid: Strong safety advisory: for tourism, Somaliland is the only realistic recommendation in this file; Mogadishu should be treated as essential-only travel with pre-arranged security, fixed hotel transfers, and no independent movement after dark.
Jilaal from December to March is the best dry-season window; April to May and October to November are hotter, dustier, or rain-disrupted enough to make road days and beach plans less reliable.

Do not assume Somaliland and Mogadishu logistics are interchangeable: flight routings, immigration handling, and airline transit rules can change quickly, so book through hotels or trusted agents and keep buffer days.
stopoverNote: Hargeisa is the easiest practical tourism base: sort SIM, cash, permits, and 4x4/escort arrangements there, continue overland to Berbera, and treat Mogadishu as a separate high-security segment rather than a casual add-on.

cities.name: Mogadishu
cities.notes: High-security capital stop for Liido Beach, the cathedral ruins, and short guided drives between hotel compounds; not the recommended leisure base compared with Somaliland.

cities.name: Hargeisa
cities.notes: Somaliland's main air gateway and the practical base for Laas Geel permits, the War Memorial, Hargeisa Cultural Centre, and onward road trips to Berbera.

cities.name: Berbera
cities.notes: Historic Red Sea port for Berbera old town, waterfront fish meals, easier beach time than Mogadishu, and the most relaxed stop in a Somaliland-first trip.

itinerary.sim: Telesom is the easiest default in Hargeisa and Berbera, while Hormuud is stronger in Mogadishu; starter packs and top-ups usually work out to about ₹300–₹900, but buy and activate in the city where you land because Somaliland and south-central Somalia run on different vendor networks.

Hargeisa.name: Hargeisa
Hargeisa.note: Base yourself in Hargeisa for permits, money exchange, and the easiest hotel choice, then treat Laas Geel as a planned daytime excursion rather than an improvised half-day hop.

days.theme: War Memorial, Cultural Centre, and market streets to settle into Somaliland's most straightforward visitor base

activities.name: Hargeisa Cultural Centre entry or event donation
Waaheen Market and central-city taxi hops

days.theme: Laas Geel rock-art day with permit, guide, and the mandatory out-of-town escort

activities.name: Shared 4x4, driver-guide, and SPU escort for the Laas Geel run
activities.tip: Solo travelers often pay much more because the vehicle and escort cost are spread across fewer people.

activities.name: Tea stop or camel-market detour on the return into Hargeisa

Berbera.name: Berbera
Berbera.note: Berbera is the most relaxed segment of the trip, but it still works best with a prearranged car from Hargeisa, daylight-only movement, and hotels that can help if plans shift.

days.theme: Ottoman-era lanes, corniche time, and a slow walk through the old port city

activities.name: Optional local guide for the Ottoman and colonial quarter
Taxi between hotel, old town, and seafront

days.theme: Fresh seafood, Gulf of Aden light, and a second day that keeps Berbera worthwhile instead of a rushed transit stop

activities.name: Berbera fish market and harbor-front wander
Private car or 4x4 half-day for out-of-town beach viewpoints
Beach chair, tea, or simple waterfront café spend

Mogadishu.name: Mogadishu
Mogadishu.note: Keep Mogadishu to a single tightly managed day inside vetted hotel and driver networks; for leisure travel, Somaliland is the stronger and safer recommendation.

days.theme: Liido Beach, cathedral ruins, and a brief look at the capital only with hotel-arranged security

activities.name: Liido Beach chair, tea, or seafood café spend
Vetted driver or security-led city circuit covering Liido, the cathedral ruins, and Hamar Weyne

connections.method: Taxi or hired 4x4 is the common tourist move; buses exist, but most foreign visitors use a private vehicle, especially if combining Laas Geel or needing flexible escort stops
connections.cost: ₹8,500–₹11,000 per taxi; ₹10,500–₹14,500 for a 4x4 with detours; buses roughly ₹400–₹850 pp

connections.method: Most visitors return overland to Hargeisa and then use a domestic air segment on Jubba Airways or another Somali carrier when schedules line up; do not assume same-day seamless transits because Somaliland/Somalia handling can change quickly
connections.cost: ₹8,500–₹11,000 back to Hargeisa by taxi plus about ₹8,500–₹15,500 airfare

extras: For leisure travelers, plan Somaliland only if you want the safest version of this destination; Mogadishu belongs in the trip only when you already have trusted security and fully pre-booked movements.
Laas Geel has no serious onsite food infrastructure, so carry water, sun protection, and exact cash for guide tips, tea stops, or unplanned checkpoint waits.
Liido Beach, Mogadishu Cathedral ruins, and Berbera old town are all effectively free to enter, but the real cost difference is transport and security rather than ticketing.
December to March is the sweet spot because Jilaal keeps Somaliland roads drier, Berbera beach weather clearer, and long daylight drives easier to manage.
